{{Quote|I think this has passed beyond a simple custody dispute, and those involved are just using it to push their own agendas, using that poor woman as a political tool.|Hraashi Lool, when asked for a comment about the "Baby Ludi" custody case|<ref name="Jedi Watch" />}}
'''Hraashi Lool''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who was attending [[University/Legends|university]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="Jedi Watch" /> in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="Date">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|46|jediwatch/1337_1.html|Jedi Refuse to Hand Over Baby Ludi}} is set on the date 13:3:7, which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], according to the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> They had [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that fell past their shoulders, green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and light skin that was colored with two vertical pink stripes. Each stripe passed over an eye as it stretched from forehead to cheek. On at least one occasion, Lool wore a tight-fitting yellow [[Necklace/Legends|necklace]] and green [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]].<ref name="Jedi Watch" />

While on a public street one [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Loor was asked by a [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition]] [[Journalist/Legends|journalist]] for their opinion on the ongoing [["Baby Ludi" custody case]], which saw the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] refuse to return a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Human [[Youngling/Legends|infant]] named [[Aris-Del Wari]] to her [[Parent/Legends|mother]], [[Jonava Billane]]. Lool opined that Billane was being used as a [[Politics/Legends|political]] tool by different factions seeking to push their own agendas.<ref name="Jedi Watch" />

==Behind the scenes==
Hraashi Lool first appeared in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 46|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #46]], an issue of the representation of the [[in-universe]] HoloNet News, published on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]]<ref name="Jedi Watch" /> on [[March 7]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN47 date">Per {{JCFCite|url=posts/6042498|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=IAmTheDarkSide|date=2002-03-14|time=5:40 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20220914072556/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-4#post-6042498}}, [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 47|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #47]] was released on [[March 14]], [[2002]]. As [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] segments were published weekly, and {{HoloNetNewsWeb|46|jediwatch/1337_1.html|Jedi Refuse to Hand Over Baby Ludi}} was published in the issue immediately preceding the forty-seventh edition, it must have been released on [[March 7]] of the same year.</ref> Lool was created by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]],<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|text=HoloNet News - Credits}}</ref> and they were pictured in an article entitled "Jedi Refuse to Hand Over Baby Ludi,"<ref name="Jedi Watch" /> which was illustrated by artist [[Joe Corroney]].<ref name="Credits" />
